speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] First up on the Green Desk - Finland's found a way to heat entire industries using nothing but sand. The Nordic nation's cracked industrial heating with sand-based thermal storage, tackling one-fifth of the world's energy consumption in the process.

speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] And Portugal's had a cracker of a month - 80.7% of their electricity came from renewables in January. That's their best record in nine months and puts them second in Europe behind Norway. Fair dinkum, the Iberian Peninsula's gone green in a big way.

speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] Meanwhile, Japan's celebrating - they've officially removed the red-crowned crane from their threatened species list after growing the population from near-extinction to nearly 2,000 birds. Guess even cranes know how to stage a comeback. Back to you legends at the desk.
